meta_title: For Founders: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Passive Income to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Learn how to generate passive income and overcome the 'can't finish' cycle. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid MVP success.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'can't finish' cycle, pivot from building complex apps to modular, micro-SaaS passive income streams. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous feature implementation, you reduce time-to-market, ensuring your MVP reaches users before your motivation wanes. Ship faster to validate revenue sooner.
content.intro:
The graveyard of failed startups is paved with 'almost finished' projects. You spend weeks on architecture, months on polish, and then the dopamine hit fades, leaving you with a half-baked repository and a sinking feeling of defeat. This isn't a failure of skill; it is a failure of system design.

True founders don't build projects; they build revenue-generating assets. By shifting your focus toward passive income micro-services, you transform the development process from a grueling marathon into a series of high-velocity sprints. Let’s dismantle the 'can't finish' syndrome using modern AI-driven development workflows.

steps.title: Define a Narrow Value Proposition
steps.description: Stop building platforms. Build a solution for one specific, painful problem. This keeps scope creep at bay.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Help me define a micro-SaaS MVP that solves a single, high-pain problem for small business owners. Keep the scope limited to a 48-hour build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project spec document for a micro-SaaS that solves [Problem] with a focus on subscription monetization.'

steps.title: Initialize the Lean Scaffold
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate your core project structure. Don't waste time on boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, production-ready Next.js 14 project structure with Stripe integration and Supabase authentication. Focus on a minimalist UI.

steps.title: Implement the Core Revenue Loop
steps.description: Build the mechanism that creates value first. If it doesn't process a payment or provide data, it isn't part of the MVP.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for a Stripe checkout session handler. Include error handling and a post-payment webhook listener.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the Stripe Checkout logic in app/api/checkout/route.ts based on the generated spec.'

steps.title: Automate Data Processing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate the 'passive' aspect of your income by hooking into an AI API.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer that takes user input, processes it via OpenAI API, and saves the result to the Supabase database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a server action in lib/actions.ts to handle AI processing and database persistence.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Get your code into a production environment immediately. Perfection is the enemy of profit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for deploying a Next.js app to Vercel with environment variables for Stripe and Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project files and suggest any critical security fixes before deployment.'

pro_tips: Always build the payment flow before the UI; if nobody pays, the UI doesn't matter.
Limit your MVP scope to exactly three screens: Landing, Checkout, and Dashboard.
Use AI-generated unit tests to ensure your logic holds up while you sleep.
If you feel stuck on a feature for more than 4 hours, delete it; it's not core to the MVP.
Leverage existing SaaS boilerplates to save 20+ hours of setup time.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, spend 80% of your time on distribution and 20% on incremental feature updates. Monitor your Stripe dashboard for the first transaction to validate your market hypothesis.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy to stop building and start earning. Don't let another project languish in your GitHub folder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
